channel.title: Software Architecture on CEDIS
channel.description: Recent content in Software Architecture on CEDIS

item.title: Products
item.description: Web application developed as a bachelor’s thesis with academic support from CEDIS for gamified learning about code smells, combining conceptual guides, interactive exercises, hints, treats, statistics, and progression.

item.title: Software Architecture
item.description: Software Architecture is a fundamental field in systems engineering involving the structural organization of software components, their interactions, and the guiding principles that define the design and evolution of systems. This area ensures the efficiency, scalability, and maintainability of software systems, directly impacting the quality and performance of applications.

item.title: People
item.description: Adjunct Professor at the University of Brasília (UnB), working in the undergraduate Software Engineering program and the Graduate Program in Applied Computing.

item.title: Paper on DevDog accepted at the SBES 2026 Tools Track
item.description: A paper stemming from Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior's bachelor's thesis was accepted at SBES 2026, supervised by Prof. Sergio Freitas and co-supervised by Prof. André Lanna.

item.title: DevDog: undergraduate thesis turns code smells into gamified learning
item.description: Luciano Ricardo da Silva Junior defended at FCTE/UnB an undergraduate thesis that gave rise to DevDog, a gamified web application for teaching code smell identification.

item.title: DevOps under the DORA lens: a portrait from the financial sector
item.description: A dissertation defended at UnB’s Applied Computing graduate program proposes a framework to assess DevOps maturity in banking teams by combining DORA metrics, document analysis and sociotechnical triangulation.

item.title: AgroMart migrates from IaaS to serverless: student duo shows small farmers can stay inside the free tier
item.description: Kalebe Cunha and Murilo Santana defended in February 2025 at UnB’s Engineering Faculty an undergraduate thesis that migrates AgroMart from IaaS to AWS Lambda and tests the feasibility of running the app within the free tier — dropping cost for farmers.

item.title: Early stage startups and software maintenance an undergraduate thesis documents the state of practice
item.description: Filipe Toyoshima Silva defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis mapping how early-stage startups handle software maintenance — a phase dominated by scarce resources and high change volume.

item.title: Who wrote that commit? An undergraduate thesis computes authorship rates inside Git projects
item.description: Laércio Silva de Sousa Júnior defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis investigating ways to compute developer authorship rates inside Git-versioned projects.

item.title: Drivers as sensors: an undergraduate thesis proposes a crowdsourced app to monitor city fleets
item.description: Jonathan Paiva and Luis Henrique Guimarães defended at UnB Gama an undergraduate thesis replacing sensors and cameras with human collaboration to monitor cars across a city — the NotifiCar app.
